zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall於大約1287年出生在英國斯塔福爾維倫哈爾時，他的父親John Leveson24歲，母親Mrs Johnna Leveson23歲。他於大約1312年與Margery Clements在英國英格蘭斯塔福爾沃爾維哈普頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1347年在他的家鄉享年61歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall于大约1287年出生在英国斯塔福爾維倫哈爾时，他的父亲John Leveson24岁，母亲Mrs Johnna Leveson23岁。他于大约1312年与Margery Clements在英国英格兰斯塔福爾沃爾維哈普頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1347年在他的家乡享年61岁。

nameMeanings.name: Leveson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1 English: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Leveson, Loveson (Old English Lēofsunu).

variants.name: Levison
meanings: English: variant of Leveson, from the Middle English personal name Leveson, Loveson (Old English Lēofsunu, from lēof ‘dear, beloved’ + sunu ‘son’).

variants.name: Lewison
meanings: Welsh and English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name   Lewis   (in Wales this was taken as equivalent to the Welsh personal name   Llewelyn  ). In North America, this surname has reportedly absorbed in some cases the Greek patronymic   Iliopoulos  .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): patronymic from the personal name Lewi, Polish-influenced form of Levy .

variants.name: Leverson
meanings: Altered form of Jewish   Levison   or   Levinson  .

variants.name: Lowson
meanings: 1 English, Scottish: relationship name from the Middle English personal name Low, a pet form of Laurence ( see Lawrence ) + -son. Compare Lawson , Larson , and Lorrison .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall was born about 1287, in Willenhall, Staffordshire, England, his father, John Leveson, was 24 and his mother, Mrs Johnna Leveson, was 23. He married Margery Clements about 1312, in Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 2 daughters. He died in 1347, in his hometown, at the age of 61.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall est né environ 1287, à Willenhall, Staffordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Leveson, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Mrs Johnna Leveson, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Margery Clements environ 1312, à Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé en 1347, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 61 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall um 1287 in Willenhall, Staffordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Leveson 24 und seine Mutter Mrs Johnna Leveson 23. Er hat um 1312 in Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, England, United Kingdom Margery Clements geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb 1347 im Alter von 61 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall è nato il circa 1287 nel Willenhall, Staffordshire, England, il suo padre, John Leveson, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Mrs Johnna Leveson, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Margery Clements circa 1312, nel Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 2 figlie. Morì nel 1347, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 61 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Richard Leveson II , of Willenhallが約1287年にイギリススタフォードシュアイアウィレンハルで生まれた時，父John Levesonは24歳，母Mrs Johnna Levesonは23歳であった。約1312年にイギリスイギリススタフォードシュアイアウオルバーハンプトンでMargery Clementsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1347年に故郷で61歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall(이)가 대략 1287년 영국 스태퍼드실르 윌렌홀에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Leveson(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs Johnna Leveson(은)는 23세였다. 그는 대략 1312년 영국 잉글랜드 스태퍼드실르 울버함프턴에서 Margery Clements(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1347년 그의 고향에서 61세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall nasceu aproximadamente 1287, em Willenhall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Leveson, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Mrs Johnna Leveson, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Margery Clements aproximadamente 1312, em Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1347, em sua cidade natal, com 61 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall родился около 1287 г. в местности уилленолл, стаффордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Leveson было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Mrs Johnna Leveson, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Margery Clements около 1312 г. в местности волвирамптон, стаффордшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер в 1347 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 61 год.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Richard Leveson II , of Willenhall nació aproximadamente 1287, en Willenhall, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Leveson, tenía 24 años y su mamá, Mrs Johnna Leveson, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Margery Clements aproximadamente 1312, en Wolverhampton, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió en 1347, en su pueblo, a la edad de 61 años.
